name: capacity_planning_v1
description: adapterOS capacity planning training data: VRAM budgeting with detailed arithmetic, throughput estimation for Apple Silicon and GPU hardware, adapter slot calculations, vertical vs horizontal scaling decisions, and infrastructure growth projections for multi-tenant deployments.

entries.notes: 2000 examples across five categories: vram_budgeting (400), throughput_estimation (400), adapter_capacity (400), scaling_decisions (400), growth_projections (400). Every example includes concrete calculations with explicit arithmetic, hardware-specific parameters, and adapterOS-specific details like KV cache sizing, adapter hot-swap headroom, and tiered adapter loading.

provenance.review_notes: All VRAM figures, throughput numbers, and hardware specs sourced from Apple Silicon published specifications and empirical adapterOS benchmarks. Calculations are fully worked with intermediate steps shown. Adapter sizing uses the rank-to-bytes formulas from adapteros-lora-worker. KV cache sizing uses the per-token-per-layer formula from adapteros-lora-worker/kvcache.rs.

evaluation_gates: all VRAM calculations show intermediate arithmetic steps, not just final answers
model sizes match known parameter-to-bytes ratios (fp16 = 2 bytes/param, 4-bit = 0.5 bytes/param)
KV cache calculations use 512 bytes/token/layer for fp16 or state the quantization explicitly
adapter sizes are consistent with LoRA rank formulas (rank × hidden_dim × 2 × target_modules × dtype_bytes)
throughput estimates cite the hardware platform and quantization level
scaling decisions include break-even analysis or cost comparison, not just qualitative advice
growth projections include at least two time horizons with concrete numbers
hot-swap headroom is accounted for in any deployment that mentions adapter swapping
intent: Teach adapters to perform accurate, hardware-aware capacity planning for adapterOS deployments. Each example should enable an operator to make concrete infrastructure decisions with real numbers rather than vague guidance.

subcategories.vram_budgeting: Given model size, adapter count, concurrency, and hardware constraints, calculate total VRAM requirements with full arithmetic breakdown. Covers fp16, 4-bit, and 8-bit quantization. Includes base model weights, LoRA adapter weights, KV cache, OS overhead, and hot-swap headroom.
subcategories.throughput_estimation: Given model, hardware, quantization, and request patterns, calculate achievable tokens/sec, latency percentiles, and maximum sustainable request rate. Covers single-stream and batched inference.
subcategories.adapter_capacity: Calculate how many LoRA adapters can be loaded simultaneously given a VRAM budget. Includes tiered loading strategies (hot/warm/cold), eviction policies, and the interaction between adapter count and KV cache budget.
subcategories.scaling_decisions: When to scale vertically (bigger hardware) vs horizontally (more nodes). Break-even cost analysis, latency implications, state management complexity. Covers Apple Silicon tiers and cloud GPU options.
subcategories.growth_projections: Plan infrastructure for growing deployments: tenant count increases, adapter proliferation, training volume growth. Includes timeline-based projections with concrete VRAM/compute/storage budgets at each milestone.
